result.frontend_typeerror_observed: TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading '0') at 0m797_i69-2c~.js:3:47929 at i_ (113b2qcua4vkz.js:50:58662) at oO (113b2qcua4vkz.js:50:78919) at oG (113b2qcua4vkz.js:50:77368) at lu (113b2qcua4vkz.js:50:94107) at uf (113b2qcua4vkz.js:50:133761) at 113b2qcua4vkz.js:50:133606 at ud (113b2qcua4vkz.js:50:133614) at ue (113b2qcua4vkz.js:50:129535) at uW (113b2qcua4vkz.js:50:155312) at MessagePort.O (113b2qcua4vkz.js:50:3470)
result.typeerror_field_name_inferred: Most likely the SSE chunk-split array (e.g., line.split(':')[0] or data.split('\n\n')[0]) is undefined. Error site 0m797_i69-2c~.js:3:47929 is at the SSE PARSER ENTRY, NOT the message schema. Schema-level fields (id/role) never reached because the SSE framing itself is being rejected (DevTools EventStream parser ALSO returns 0 rows = malformed SSE).
result.comparison_to_605: v2 (id+role) did NOT fix the [0] TypeError -- same exact error, same stack signature (113b2qcua4vkz.js:50 chain), EventStream tab still empty (0 events). The fix was applied at the wrong layer; problem is at SSE wire format level, not envelope schema fields.
result.next_field_to_add_recommendation:
STOP adding schema fields. The SSE wire format itself is malformed. DevTools EventStream parser shows 0 events even though response is 200/1.1kB. Required actions: (1) Capture the actual envelope bytes via etap.log binary dump, (2) Compare side-by-side with the verbatim ground-truth response in files/604/api_response_body.txt, (3) Verify each event has 'event: <name>
data: <json>

' structure with TWO newlines between events (CRLF or LF, but consistent), (4) Verify 'event:' and 'data:' line prefixes are present (no missing or trailing whitespace), (5) Verify JSON value on 'data:' line is on a single line (no embedded newlines that would split it across multiple data: continuations), (6) Verify Content-Type header is exactly 'text/event-stream;charset=UTF-8' (case-sensitive matching, with charset). Likely root cause: missing blank-line separator between events, OR missing 'data: ' prefix on the JSON line, OR JSON is being base64-encoded / wrapped in unexpected envelope by APF emitter.

screenshots_inventory.description: Error popup '일시적인 오류가 발생했어요' overlay covering chat area, no warning rendered (same as #605)
EventStream tab empty (0 rows in Id/Type/Data columns) for /v1/rooms/.../messages/stream POST 200 1.1kB -- SSE parser rejected response despite valid Content-Type header
Console TypeError 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 0)' with full stack trace through 113b2qcua4vkz.js:50 (SSE handler) -- IDENTICAL signature to #605

result.diagnostic_for_dev_team: Cycle 94 R-26 zeta_sse_v2 verification: PROBLEM IS NOT SCHEMA. The v1->v2 changes (replyMessage.messageId -> id, added role:assistant) did NOT change the failure mode at all -- DevTools EventStream parser still parses 0 events, frontend still throws the exact same TypeError 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading [0])' with identical stack signature, UI still shows same error popup. This means the failure is at the SSE WIRE FORMAT level, before any JSON schema is even examined. CRITICAL DIAGNOSIS: DevTools EventStream parser is browser-built, not app code -- if it shows 0 events for a 1.1kB body with Content-Type text/event-stream, then the SSE framing itself is malformed. NOT a schema problem. Recommend the dev team STOP iterating on field names/values and instead: (a) dump actual envelope bytes from etap.log, (b) byte-diff vs files/604/api_response_body.txt, (c) verify event line/data line prefixes + blank-line separators + Content-Type header. The /v1/rooms/.../messages/stream is hit at 14ms (very fast = APF intercept confirmed firing), 1126 bytes returned, but parser rejects framing entirely. v3 should be a wire-format fix (not a schema fix).
